title: Builder Traits Snapshot

introduction.subtitle: A five-axis personality snapshot for venture builders, using public-domain IPIP items.
introduction.description:
There is no single type of founder. Personality only explains part of how someone builds — but it shapes what drains you, what you avoid, and where you need a complement. This snapshot maps five traits relevant to venture work, not as scores to maximize, but as positions on axes that each have a cost and a use.

Answer about how you actually are, not how you wish to be. Both poles of every axis have value depending on context.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: This is a short reflective snapshot, not a clinical or validated psychometric assessment. It uses public-domain IPIP items but is not the full IPIP scale.

conceptHelp.title: Openness: I have a vivid imagination.
conceptHelp.summary: Openness means curiosity, imagination, tolerance for novelty, and interest in abstract or unconventional ideas. This statement is one small behavioral signal for that pattern: "I have a vivid imagination.".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Self-knowledge items are useful when they help you notice repeated behavior. They become misleading if one answer is treated as a permanent identity or diagnosis.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think across normal weeks, not one unusually good or bad day. Rate the statement by how often it describes your real preference or behavior.
conceptHelp.example: A high answer means this statement is regularly true for you; a low answer means it is rare, context-specific, or not how you usually operate.

attribution.text: Personality items from the International Personality Item Pool (ipip.ori.org), public domain.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
